content:
4 min read
Just now

--

So, who does not like watching movies? That should not be a question though right.
Movies have evolved from mere entertainment into a vital, almost indispensable part of modern life, shaping our thoughts, emotions, and perceptions in profound ways. They serve as a collective escape, offering a temporary refuge from the stresses of daily routine, while simultaneously acting as a powerful mirror to society, reflecting our shared hopes, fears, and values.
It actively rewires our thinking, challenging our preconceived notions, sparking introspection, and planting seeds of inspiration that can influence our personal decisions and worldview. Whether it’s a thought-provoking documentary that shifts our perspective on a global issue or a simple romantic comedy that reminds us of the importance of connection, movies have become an essential language through which we understand ourselves and the complex world around us.
There are so many platforms that provides the convenience of watching any movies, series, OTTs and many more varieties of entertainment but i am sure this must have happened to almost everyone where you’d watched a movie and you felt perhaps you should have watched something else than that “movie” which your friends(2–3) liked a lottt. Or maybe you should have done your research beforehand which includes searching up the movie, scrolling to various websites to see reviews or what not. Personally, that “research” thing would ruin my whole mood and most probably I would end up NOT watching any movie.
You see time is very precious to everyone, no one would want to spend so much time watching a certain movie, just for that to end up ruining their entire saturday evening afterwards. The process of searching itself is so overwhelming due to too many yes(s) and no(s) that one’s dopamine and excitement fails to increase.
So, to keep you hooked due to failing attention span
My team CakePops, decided to create a movie searching website where all you have to do is to search the title of a movie and kaboom! You will get all the necessary details regarding that movie at one place. No need to do endless scrolling which leads to nothingness and ends up wasting time.
SilverScreen is going to be an all-in-one movie hunt where a user can personalised theme settings based on mood, interactive components, and underwhelming experience of endless searching. It is curated to adhere to users expectations and preferences as our main goal.
<-The Technological Stack->
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Fig:- Tech stack of SilverScreen: DOM References
— DOM stands for “Document Object Model” is a programming interface for web documents that represents the page structure as a tree of objects. allowing languages like JavaScript to read and change the document style, structure, and content.
DOM methods and properties we used in our application:-
a) Selecting Elements-Methods used to locate and target elements within the document tree
const searchInput = document.getElementById('searchInput');
const watchlistItems = document.getElementById('watchlistItems');
const themeToggle = document.getElementById('themeToggle');b) Managing Styles and Classes-Properties used to interact directly with an element’s presentation layer
function toggleTheme() {
document.body.classList.toggle('light-mode');
const isLight = document.body.classList.contains('light-mode');
localStorage.setItem('ghibli-theme', isLight ? 'light' : 'dark');
}: JSON
— JSON(JavaScript Object Notation) is lightweight, text-based data interchange format used to store and transport structured data that is both human-readable and machine-parsable.

:API
— Application Programming Interface(API) is a set of protocols and rules that allows different software systems to communicate and exchange data with each other. It acts as a secure bridge, letting an application request specific services or information from another without exposing its internal code or database.
Like, here we have used Google Font APIs to link it to our HTML document.
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Quicksand:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet" />~For deployment purposes, Render is used in the website.
